name: fix-the-video-editor-s-20260325-1831
idea: Fix the video editor's OverlayEditor (preview drag/resize) system. Three issues: (1) Moving a clip on the preview changes its aspect ratio/shape when it should maintain dimensions — only corner resize should change size. (2) Need edge dragging for cropping (left/right/top/bottom edges crop the clip) vs corner dragging for resize. (3) Ghost/shadow artifact when dragging clips on the timeline — something visual lingers until drop. Investigate whether these are symptomatic of poor structure in OverlayEditor.tsx and related code, or just incremental fixes. Goal: make preview overlay interactions predictable and clean — move preserves shape, corners resize, edges crop, no visual artifacts.

overrides.reason: User clarified: (1) Ghost/shadow artifact is on the PREVIEW OVERLAY (OverlayEditor.tsx), NOT the timeline. When dragging a clip around on the preview canvas, something visual lingers — not related to useClipDrag.ts or useTimelineEditing.ts at all. (2) Crop visibility: fully hidden normally, but when the asset is clicked/selected, show a dimmed ghost of the full uncropped area so the user can see what they're working with. (3) All three issues are strictly within the preview overlay system (OverlayEditor.tsx and related), not the timeline.

notes.note: T3/T4 were completely misdirected at timeline drag code. Refocus entirely on OverlayEditor.tsx preview interactions. The ghost is likely related to the drag preview rendering in OverlayEditor itself — possibly the live drag overlay (lines 327-359) or how bounds are rendered during drag. For crop UX: selected clip shows full uncropped bounds as a dimmed ghost behind the visible cropped area.

last_gate.rationale: Weighted score dropped from 3.25 to 0.5 — strong improvement. Two of three original flags resolved. The remaining flag (crop persistence round-trip in timeline-data.ts) is a concrete, localized executor task: copy crop fields in getDefaultClipMeta and the serialization mapping. The plan already names timeline-data.ts in Step 4; the gap is just that it doesn't enumerate the two specific mapping sites. This is execution-level detail, not a planning flaw. All three bug fixes (string matching, coordinate scaling, ghost removal) are well-analyzed with clear root causes. The crop feature chain is coherent. No value in another iteration.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Score trajectory 3.25 → 0.5 shows rapid convergence. Two significant flags resolved (crop structure and ghost over-engineering). One remaining flag is narrow (two mapping sites in timeline-data.ts need crop fields copied). No recurring critiques, no scope creep, preflight clean. Plan is focused entirely on OverlayEditor.tsx and related files per user redirect.
warnings: crop-persistence-misses-serialization-path: Executor must update BOTH getDefaultClipMeta (~line 101-123) AND the serialization mapping (~line 234-291) in timeline-data.ts, not just the ClipMeta interface. Without both, crop won't round-trip.

settled_decisions.decision: Move bug root cause is string-matching: 'move'.includes('e') === true. Fix with else branch guard.
settled_decisions.rationale: Verified from code. No alternative explanation.

settled_decisions.decision: Ghost artifact is the intentional dragOriginStyle div (lines 297-313), not leaked DOM. Remove it in Phase 1, repurpose concept for crop ghost in Phase 2.
settled_decisions.rationale: User confirmed ghost is in preview overlay, not timeline. Code inspection confirms explicit rendering of dashed-border origin div.

settled_decisions.decision: Crop uses clip-path: inset() on VisualClip, not rendering pipeline restructure.
settled_decisions.rationale: Avoids structural changes to the single-rectangle-per-clip model. clip-path works in both preview and Remotion render.

settled_decisions.decision: Execution order: Phase 1 (T1-T3 move/resize/ghost fixes) then Phase 2 (T4-T7 crop feature).
settled_decisions.rationale: Fixes existing bugs first with zero new surface area before adding crop feature.

settled_decisions.decision: Edge crop handles only on non-text clips.
settled_decisions.rationale: Text clips have their own editing interactions; crop is a media-only concept.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: First iteration; follow gate recommendation: PROCEED. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.
